groups.context_description: Term names the disruption or perceived disruption of rational inner faculties — being out of one's mind, whether genuinely or in others' assessment
Term names the considered judgment or opinion of a person — an inner resolution or assessment offered with authority
Term names a shared inner purpose — collective resolution placed in hearts by God or formed among persons toward a common end
Term names the state of mental derangement — the collapse of rational inner faculties attributed to excessive learning
Term names unity of inner orientation — a shared disposition of mind and spirit among the community
Term names a tender, friendly inner disposition — the mind oriented toward others with gentleness and courtesy
Term names an inner faculty of the person — the deep inner life searched and assessed by Christ
Term names human inner thought and planning — the purposive deliberation of the heart, whether toward good or evil, faithful or rebellious ends
Term names the divine thoughts and purposes — God's inner deliberative will that exceeds human understanding and stands forever
Term names the evil thoughts that originate in the heart — the corrupt inner reasoning that produces sin and estrangement from God
Term names inner doubts, arguments, or disputed opinions — the mind's questioning and deliberating about contested matters
Term names sound-mindedness as an inner quality — the rational, morally disciplined disposition of the person, whether in speech or conduct
Term names the creative inner faculty of design — the skilled devising of form, pattern, or means, whether in craftsmanship or providential mercy
Term names inner scheming — the deliberate devising of plans directed against another
Term names the inner capacity for explanation and calculation — the rational faculty seeking to understand the scheme of things
Term names the commanded human act of remembering — calling to mind God's acts, his covenant, one's own ways, and those in need, as the ground of present faithfulness
Term names divine remembering — God holding persons, his covenant, and his people in active attentiveness, with grace or judgment as the result
Term names the inner act of reasoning or questioning — the mind deliberating with itself or the heart raising doubts and questions
Term names deliberation and discussion among persons — reasoning together about a matter
Term names the inner faculty of thought — the mind's contents and its susceptibility to hardening, blinding, or corrupting by spiritual forces
Term names inner division of allegiance — the person whose loyalty is split between God and the world, producing instability
Term names shared inner disposition — genuine concern and aligned soul-orientation toward others
Term names the inner act of deliberate decision — the mind resolving upon a course of action through weighing and choosing
Term names deliberate planning directed toward harm — the inner will forming an intention against another
Term names the faculty of mature thinking — the capacity for moral and rational discernment that marks adult inner life
Term names the reversal of inner judgment — changing one's assessment of a person or situation
Term names troubled, disquieting thoughts — the inner agitation of the mind under pressure or in visionary states
Term names the freewill inner disposition — voluntary offering or willingness expressed in dedication to God's house
Term names the inner disposition of willing, voluntary devotion — the heart spontaneously moved to give or serve without compulsion
Term names the deliberate devising of harm — the inner formation of schemes, plots, or evil plans against another
Term names purposive planning — the inner formation of a deliberate purpose or plan, whether by God or human agents
Term names the moral crediting or imputing of righteousness/guilt — the inner act of reckoning a person's standing before God or human authority
Term names the evaluative regard of persons or things — the inner act of assessing worth, status, or character in relational and social contexts
Term names the inner act of thinking and considering — the mind forming judgments, reconsidering paths, or holding deliberate thoughts
Term names the inner evaluative act of counting or regarding — assessing a person's or thing's worth, status, or standing
Term names the product of human ingenuity — devices or schemes formed by the inner inventive capacity, whether for construction or moral harm
Term names the inner act of forgetting or neglecting — the failure to retain in active consciousness what matters morally or relationally
Term names the inner thoughts and purposes of the heart — the hidden intentional life that only God's word can discern and expose
Term names the institutional memorial — an act, object, or day established to keep an event or person before God and the community across generations
Term names the priestly/liturgical keeping of persons before God — the act of bearing names or offerings into God's presence for ongoing remembrance
Term names the inner act of remembrance — the capacity to hold persons and events in living consciousness, and the absence of such memory as loss
Term names the memorial portion of an offering — the part burned before God as an act of bringing the offerer to divine remembrance
Term names the inner act of setting attention — directing the mind to consider, take to heart, or attend carefully to a matter or person
Term names purposive formation — the inner act of planning or forming a purpose, whether divine sovereign intention or human scheme
Term names the mode of expression that departs from rational restraint — speaking beyond reason, used rhetorically to mark apostolic boasting as a concession
Term names the madness of disordered inner impulse — the prophet's irrational compulsion rebuked by God through a donkey
Term names self-control as an inner character quality — the disciplined mind that governs its impulses, required for godly leadership and life
Term names the formation of inner dispositions through training — shaping the affective and relational life toward godly patterns
Term names the inner disposition of self-controlled living — the mind governing conduct with measured restraint
Term names the commanded inner disposition of sober alertness — the mind clear and watchful, not given to spiritual or moral drowsiness
Term names inner division — the split allegiance of the person who cannot commit fully to God's law
Term names the act of reminding as a pastoral instrument — stirring up the inner life by bringing truth back to active consciousness
Term names the prompting of inner remembrance — bringing truth or prior words back into active inner awareness through the Spirit, an apostle, or another person
Term names sobriety of mind as a character quality — the inner disposition of measured, clear-headed judgment required for leadership
Term names madness or derangement — the inner disruption of reason and moral orientation, whether feigned, induced, or corporate
Term names boasting arrogance as an inner orientation — the exaltation of self before God and others
Term names raving madness or contemptuous derision — the expression of disordered inner intensity, whether in mockery, futility, or frenzied action
Term names madness as an inner condition — the disordered, futile orientation of the human heart and mind, investigated alongside wisdom
Term names madness as the culmination of foolishness — the final, evil expression of a disordered inner life
Term names the inner disposition of covenantal obedience — the will, heart, and soul directed toward faithful keeping of God's commands across generations
Term names God's guarding of his people — the divine inner disposition of protective care that preserves, shields, and keeps those who belong to him
Term names self-keeping from moral harm — the inner discipline of guarding one's ways, mouth, and soul from sin and its consequences
Term names attentive moral observation — watching over conduct, marking iniquity, or directing inner regard toward a person or object with moral consequence
Term names careful, attentive inner vigilance — the disposition of watchfulness toward one's conduct, duties, or dangers, especially in relation to God's commands
Term names repentance as the inner-being transformation — the godly grief that leads to reorientation of life toward God, given as a divine gift and leading to forgiveness and life
Term names the commanded inner act of remembrance — calling to mind persons, words, or events as the basis for present faith and conduct
Term names the inner orientation of remembrance as motivating force — thinking of, being concerned with, or holding in mind as ground of present action
Term names the capacity for recall — the ability to bring to active consciousness what has been received
Term names human remembrance — the inner act of bringing to mind what was said, experienced, or promised
Term names divine remembrance — God holding a person or their deeds in active attentiveness, with grace or judgment as the result
Term names God's chosen non-remembrance — the new covenant promise to remember sins no more
Term names affectionate remembrance of persons — holding them in active prayerful awareness before God
Term names the inner act of mindful attentiveness — holding another in active awareness with moral or relational consequence
Term names the inner act of bringing prior knowledge or experience back into active awareness — voluntary recall or prompted remembrance
Term names the eucharistic act of remembrance — inner and communal re-presentation of Christ's self-giving before God and one another
Term names the annual reminder of unresolved sin — the old covenant's inability to finally cleanse the conscience
Term names the human act of comforting — bringing relief to inner grief through presence, words, or care
Term names divine comfort — God actively bringing relief and restoration to the inner grief of his people
Term names human relenting — the inner change of heart toward repentance before God or reversal of a previous disposition
Term names divine relenting — God's inner-being disposition changing in response to human repentance or intercession, turning from intended judgment
Term names repentance as the inner-being act of turning — the complete reorientation of the person from sin toward God, called for by prophets, apostles, and Christ himself
Term names the inner reversal of a previous decision — the change of mind or feeling of regret that follows a prior action or stance
Term names a conscious inner awareness — moral complicity or cognitive recognition — that informs volitional response
Term names a determined purposive will — human deliberate intention or divine sovereign resolve — as the inner origin of directed action
Term names the inner act of human willing, intending, or desiring — the volitional faculty in motion toward an object or action
Term names God's or Christ's inner purposive will — sovereign divine willing that directs revelation, creation, and redemption
Term names the directing of the inner mind toward a purpose — the act of setting one's mental attention and will upon an object of concern
Term names the inner deliberative purpose or resolved intention of a person, located within the heart as the seat of purposive will
Term names God's sovereign purposive counsel that directs human events and vocation, intersecting with the inner life of those subject to it
Term names the formed inner inclination of the heart — the moral orientation shaped within the person, inclining toward evil or good
Term names the creaturely formation known and addressed by God — the constitution of the human person as formed creature, sustained or oriented in peace
Term names the inner act of remembrance — the capacity by which God, persons, or deeds are held in living consciousness; its cessation marks finality
Term names the memorial as moral legacy — the lasting presence or extinction of a person's name in communal memory, carrying moral weight
Term names the mind as capable of knowing God and serving his law — the rational-moral faculty oriented toward truth and divine will
Term names the mind as corrupted, debased, or futile — the inner rational faculty given over to moral disorder or darkened by sin
Term names the mind of the Lord/God — the divine inner rational-purposive faculty inaccessible to humans except through revelation
Term names the mind as the seat of love and orientation toward God — the whole inner capacity directed Godward in the great commandment
Term names the mind as susceptible to darkening, hostility, or moral corruption — understanding alienated from God or carrying out sinful desires
Term names the mind as the site of divine law-writing and renewal — the inner capacity transformed and inscribed by God under the new covenant
Term names the direction of the mind — setting one's inner orientation toward God, the Spirit, or godly patterns of life
Term names the mind as the seat of communal unity and personal relationship — being of the same mind, sharing concern, pursuing agreement
Term names the settled orientation of the inner mind — the deep dispositional set of the person toward flesh or Spirit, with life or death as the outcome
Term names the restoration of sound mind — the inner rational and moral faculties recovered from disruption
Term names sound-mindedness as a moral disposition — thinking about oneself and others with measured, humble judgment
Term names the soul's wholehearted consecration to God — loving, seeking, and serving God with all the soul
Term names the soul as the seat of inner emotional states — anguish, sorrow, bitterness, longing, joy
Term names the soul as the seat of desire and longing — for God, the beloved, or earthly things
Term names the divine soul — God's inner being, expressing delight in the chosen one or abhorrence of faithlessness
Term names the soul kept, guarded, and delivered — God preserving the soul from death, Sheol, and enemies
Term names the soul's moral and spiritual nourishment — what revives, refreshes, or satisfies the inner person
Term names the soul as the seat of moral character — upright or puffed up, righteous or wicked
Term names the Servant's soul poured out in self-offering — bearing anguish, making offering for guilt, poured out to death
Term names the soul's capacity for praise, worship, and inner exultation before God
Term names the soul-bond of covenant friendship — the soul knit to another's soul; the friend who is as one's own soul
Term names the soul poured out in prayer — the inner act of bringing the soul before God in intercession and confession
Term names the atonement relationship — the life of the flesh in the blood given for the souls of the people
Term names the life as precious before God and man — the infinite worth of the individual's soul-life in God's sight
Term names the life redeemed or delivered — God preserving, ransoming, or restoring the soul from death
Term names the life voluntarily offered or risked — persons risking their soul in battle, ministry, or rescue
Term names the soul/life in extremity of inner despair — the person seeking death or fainting, the inner life overwhelmed
Term names the life as the object of legal protection — the lex talionis; the prohibition on blood as the seat of life
Term names the life of every creature held in God's hand — divine sovereignty over all life
Term names the divine soul's inner disposition — God's soul not abhorring or abhorring; divine acceptance or rejection of his people
Term names the binding of the self through a vow — the inner act of committing oneself through a pledged word
Term names deliberate inner self-humbling through fasting — afflicting the self as a penitential inner act
Term names the self as subject of inner moral defilement — making oneself detestable through boundary violations
Term names inner self-giving — pouring oneself out for others in generosity
Term names divine impatience arising from compassion — God's inner response to his people's misery
Term names inner anguish and self-reflective suffering — inner experience of grief, courage dissolving
Term names the morally accountable person — the soul as the unit who must obey, bears iniquity, and faces divine judgment or blessing
Term names the human being as a living soul — constituted at creation as a living creature by the divine breath
Term names the living creature included in God's covenant — the soul-nature of animals within the scope of divine care
Term names appetite and craving as inner states that drive action — hunger and longing as expressions of deep inner need
Term names the wicked's desire for evil — inner appetite of the corrupt person oriented toward violence and iniquity
Term names the soul's desire directed toward God or what is good — inner longing toward holy or worthy objects
Term names the soul satisfied or unsatisfied — the inner state of fulfilment or continued craving
Term names the will — the inner disposition that grants permission or determines the direction of action
Term names the dead body requiring inner spiritual separation — the soul of the dead as source of contamination requiring priestly holiness
Term names overwhelming danger reaching the neck — the inner experience of being engulfed, on the verge of destruction
Term names the refreshing of the inner person through rest — restoration of the inner being through Sabbath rest
Term names the soul as the essential inner person — the non-physical self that can be forfeited, preserved, or lost; more valuable than the body
Term names the soul as the seat of inner states — sorrow, longing, pain, and rest; the inner being as the location of full emotional-spiritual experience
Term names the soul as the object of pastoral care — the inner being watched over by leaders, strengthened against weariness, saved from wandering
Term names the soul's wholehearted consecration to God — loving God with all the soul as the supreme inner act of devotion
Term names the soul in corporate unity — believers sharing one heart and soul in inner commitment
Term names the soul as the inner moral self engaged in work — doing God's will from the soul, working heartily rather than for appearance
Term names the soul as the post-mortem continuing existence — preserved under the altar, not abandoned to Hades, reigning with Christ
Term names the soul as the target of spiritual warfare — the inner being attacked by passions, poisoned, unsettled, or enticed
Term names the divine soul — God's inner being, able to be well pleased or displeased
Term names the life that is lost by clinging or found by surrendering — the paradox of self-denial as the path to one's deepest life
Term names the life laid down in love — Christ giving his life for the sheep; the supreme pattern of self-giving love unto death
Term names the soul/life not held as precious — the inner disposition of releasing one's claim on one's own life for ministry and the gospel
Term names the soul/life as the object of anxiety — the command not to be anxious about one's life as an inner disposition of trust
Term names the life at risk or under threat — the person's life sought by enemies, at stake in crisis, preserved through endurance
Term names the moral weight of saving or destroying a life — the Sabbath question as a test of inner moral priority
Term names the soul as the locus of intense inner anguish — the soul sorrowful to death, troubled before the passion
Term names the soul's capacity for worship — magnifying God at the soul level, the deepest personal act of praise
Term names the soul as the object of divine pleasure or displeasure — God's inner evaluation of the person's faith or faithlessness
Term names each person as an accountable soul — the morally responsible unit that must obey, faces judgment, and experiences inner states such as awe
Term names the natural/soul-level inner condition — operating from the soul alone, incapable of receiving spiritual truth
Term names the natural body as the lower form — sown natural, raised spiritual; soul-level as pre-resurrection mode
Term names the moment of expiry as consequence of inner moral failure — deception and pride resulting in death as divine judgment
Term names overwhelming inner fear and foreboding causing fainting — the person's inner being overtaken by eschatological terror
Term names the lifeless/soulless state of an instrument — implying the person, unlike a lifeless object, has inner life expected to communicate with intelligible meaning
Term names the fainthearted inner state — the soul diminished in courage, needing encouragement from others
Term names the inner parts as the seat of compassion and tender emotion — the physical location of deep emotional stirring
Term names the inner parts as the seat of grief and anguish — the physical location of acute inner pain
Term names the inner parts as the origin of speech and wisdom — the source from which words and understanding flow
Term names the disheartened inner state — the inner being made faint and discouraged by opposition
Term names the bosom as the seat of intimate trusted relationship — the close inner circle of family and friendship
Term names the bosom as the place of cherishing — what the inner person holds closest
Term names the bosom as the seat of hidden inner knowledge — concealing transgression within oneself rather than confessing
Term names the inner parts as the seat of the indwelling divine presence — God in the midst of his people as the most intimate location
Term names the inner parts as the seat of moral character — what lies within the person as the source of their conduct
Term names the inner parts as the location of renewal — God's new covenant work written within the person
Term names the inner parts as the location of false prophets' lies — the source of deceptive claims about God
Term names the inner parts as the seat of hostile intent — what lies within as the source of evil plotting against others
Term names the heart in its feminine/intensive form — the inner person's affection and emotional depth expressed with particular intensity
Term names the heart's wholehearted devotion to God — loving, serving, and seeking God with the entire inner person
Term names the heart as the seat of inner moral character — what the person truly is inwardly, known to God though hidden from others
Term names the heart known and searched by God — the divine reading of the innermost person
Term names the hardened or stubborn heart — the inner resistance that refuses God's word and covenant
Term names the fainting or melting heart — the inner collapse of courage and resolve through fear or judgment
Term names the prideful or exalted heart — the inner self-elevation that turns from God toward autonomy
Term names the inner speech of the heart — what the person says within themselves, forming intentions, plans, and self-assessments
Term names the heart in its experiential states — the inner register of grief, gladness, sorrow, longing, and moral reflection
Term names the circumcision and renewal of the heart — the divine transformation of the stubborn inner person toward love and obedience
Term names the heart as the place where God's word is received, laid up, and kept — the inner ground of faithful remembrance
Term names the heart in its Aramaic form — the seat of thought, will, and moral orientation in the person
Term names the heart as the essential inner person — the Aramaic heart designating the seat of thought and moral life
Term names the heart as the inner organ of understanding — where God places wisdom and discernment
Term names the inner parts as the seat of truth and wisdom — God desiring truth in the deepest places of the person
Term names the loins as the seat of strength and generative power — the source of descendants and inner fortitude
Term names the loins girded for action — the inner readiness and preparation for challenge or service
Term names the act of captivating or encouraging the heart — the inner person stirred through intimate encounter
Term names the withering of inner vigour — the fading of inner strength or courage under pressure or judgment
Term names the withering of the spirit as a moral metaphor — spiritual apostasy pictured as withering
Term names the seat of compassion — deep inner stirring of tender mercy toward another
Term names the innermost affection — the deepest bond of love described as a physical inner stirring
Term names the inward parts as the seat of Christian character qualities placed there by Christ
Term names the inner quality of compassion — the tender-hearted disposition marked by kindness and mercy
Term names hardness of heart as a moral condition — inner disposition of stubborn resistance refusing God's covenantal purposes
Term names the heart as the seat of inner moral life — the source from which character, intention, and moral choice flow
Term names the heart as the target of God's word — the inner receptive ground in which the word is sown and bears fruit
Term names the heart as the seat of belief and confession — the inner faculty through which faith is held and salvation received
Term names the heart known by God — the divine searching, testing, and reading of the inner person
Term names the hardened heart — inner resistance refusing the word, hardening against God, leading to spiritual blindness
Term names the heart in prayer and devotion — the inner orientation in worship, prayer, and seeking God
Term names the troubled or comforted heart — the inner experience of anxiety, grief, or peace
Term names the pure and single heart — the undivided inner orientation toward God marked by integrity
Term names the heart as the seat of the Spirit's indwelling — the inner person where God's love and presence are poured
Term names God as the one who knows hearts — the divine inner-being knowledge of every person's innermost reality
Term names the inner command to take heart — courage addressed to the inner life, grounded in Christ's presence and victory
Term names the inaugural inner renewal — the heart opened to new covenant access through Christ's blood
Term names the inner loss of heart — the fading of inner courage and resolve in the face of suffering or opposition
Term names the breath of life given by God — the divine neshamah constituting the inner life of the human person at creation and sustaining it
Term names the breath of the Almighty as the vehicle of understanding — neshamah as the God-given inner lamp enabling understanding
Term names the divine breath in judgment and theophany — God's breath as a force bringing death, dispersion, or judgment
Term names breath as the qualification for worship and the marker of creaturely dependence on God's gift of life
Term names the spiritual person — one whose inner life is ordered by the Spirit, capable of discerning all things, contrasted with the person of the flesh
Term names spiritual gifts and blessings — the Spirit's concrete manifestations of inner transformation and community endowment
Term names the spiritual law and body — the law as spiritual in character; the progression from natural to spiritual in resurrection
Term names the spiritual forces of evil — the dark spiritual realm opposing the inner life
Term names spiritual wisdom and understanding — inner capacity for wisdom flowing from the Spirit's work
Term names the Belial inner character — the worthless inner disposition of wickedness, plotting evil, contempt for justice
Term names the divining spirit — a false spiritual power inhabiting a person, counterfeiting prophetic inner capacity for commercial gain
Term names the mistaken perception of a ghost producing inner terror — the fear arising from false spiritual perception
Term names inner corporate unity — being same-souled, sharing one inner orientation, love, and mind in the community
Term names the Holy Spirit as the divine agent who fills, leads, empowers, and transforms the inner being of the person
Term names holiness as the inner character calling — being holy in all conduct, body, and spirit, as God is holy
Term names the blasphemy against the Holy Spirit — the inner moral act of attributing divine works to Satan; the unforgiveable disposition
Term names the Holy Spirit as capable of being grieved, lied to, or resisted — susceptible to harm through human inner choices
Term names the Holy One — Christ or God as holy; divine transcendence and moral purity as an object of inner recognition
Term names the medium as a false spiritual access point — the inner prohibition against seeking guidance through forbidden channels
Term names the desperate consultation of a medium — inner act of seeking forbidden spiritual guidance driven by despair
Term names the quality of gentleness — dealing softly and gently as an inner disposition; and the rejection of God's quiet provision
Term names inner dejection — the downcast, dejectedly-moving spirit under moral confrontation
Term names the mutterer as a false spiritual practitioner — one who whispers and mutters as a form of spiritual deception
Term names the shades as those who have lost the capacity for praise — the departed whose inner-being engagement with God has been severed by death
Term names the path of folly leading to the company of the dead — inner moral choices directing the person toward Sheol
Term names the resurrection hope — the dead of God's people shall live and rise; the inner-being of the righteous awaiting restoration
Term names the Holy Spirit as the divine transforming presence — filling, leading, interceding, bearing witness with the human spirit
Term names the human spirit as the seat of inner life — the person's spirit that prays, serves, is troubled, fervent, refreshed, or willing
Term names the spirit-flesh contrast — the spirit willing but flesh weak; inner orientation toward God vs. the flesh's orientation toward self
Term names the unclean/evil spirit — the spiritual being oppressing human persons; the inner-being impact of spiritual affliction
Term names spirit-characterised orientations — spirit of adoption, stupor, fear, power/love/self-control; shaping the person's fundamental inner disposition
Term names the life-giving Spirit and the giving up of spirit — Christ as life-giving spirit; the moment of death as yielding up of the spirit
Term names the spiritual gifts — the Spirit's varied manifestations for the common good; the distribution of the Spirit's inner workings
Term names breath/wind as a metaphor for the Spirit's sovereign invisible inner work; and divine breath as the vehicle of life and judgment
Term names the divine breath of life — God breathing into the person the breath constituting inner human existence; prophetic breath restoring life
Term names the divine breath as judgment or discipline — God blowing in wrath or covenantal intervention
Term names breath as inner vitality — fainting when inner vitality is exhausted; the life-breath connection to inner energy
Term names the fleshly heart as the tablet of new covenant writing — the Spirit inscribing the new covenant on the inner person
Term names physical/fleshly descent as insufficient — contrasted with the inner power of indestructible life
Sha.a.rah as kinship bond creating moral prohibition
Be.shar as kinship bond and personal solidarity
Be.shar as creaturely condition of humanity before God
Be.shar as domain of misplaced creaturely trust
Be.shar as site of the covenant sign: circumcision
Be.shar as body mediating and expressing inner-being states
Be.shar in prophetic transformation: heart of flesh replacing heart of stone
Ba.sar as kinship bond and personal solidarity
Ba.sar as creaturely condition of humanity before God
Ba.sar as domain of misplaced creaturely trust
Ba.sar as site of the covenant sign: circumcision
Ba.sar as body mediating and expressing inner-being states
Ba.sar in prophetic transformation: heart of flesh replacing heart of stone
She.er as flesh-blood bond of kinship and moral obligation
She.er naming the whole person alongside the heart
Ya.tsa as going-out of the soul or spirit
Ya.tsa as going-forth of Gods word, law, righteousness, or judgment
Ya.tsa as expression of inner states going outward
Ya.tsa as divine going-out: God going forth in theophanic action
Term names the inner disposition of debauchery — shameless abandonment to sensuality; the callous inner self-surrender to unbridled bodily passion
Sarx as governing principle of fallen human desire, passions, and moral incapacity
Sarx as creaturely mortal condition of embodied human existence
Sarx in Christological confession: Christs incarnate flesh
Sarx in covenantal union: one flesh as marriage bond
Term names the fleshly inner condition — the person dominated by the flesh, manifesting in jealousy, strife, and spiritual immaturity
Term names the fleshly inner orientation in conflict with the Spirit — fleshly wisdom, weapons, passions warring against the soul
Term names voluntary abstinence from meat as an act of inner love — the moral decision to forgo a right out of concern for a weaker brother's conscience
Term names the false mark of outward mutilation — contrasted implicitly with the true inner spiritual circumcision of the heart
Term names the inner disposition of arrogance — the haughtiness of the wealthy person who sets themselves above others and above God
Term names the inner disposition of haughtiness — self-elevation and valuing what is exalted among people, warned against in favour of humility and fear
Term names the inner condition of brutish insensibility — the person who is beast-like in understanding, unable or unwilling to perceive what is true
Term names the inner disposition of careful attentiveness — the thoughtful orientation of the person toward devoted action
Term names the inner disposition of thinking too highly of oneself — self-inflated assessment contrasted with sober self-knowledge
Term names the inner act of humbling oneself before God — the voluntary posture of the heart that turns from pride and seeks God's face
Term names God's act of bringing down the proud or disciplining the people — the inner condition of the person whose heart is humbled by affliction or judgment
Term names the insensitive heart — rendered unfeeling like fat, incapable of genuine response to God or his word
Term names apparent or feigned madness — the inner state or outer presentation that leads others to perceive a person as mad
Term names the dismissal of prophetic intensity as madness, or the inner disintegration produced by divine judgment
Term names madness as a condition of the inner being — whether reckless furious intensity or the madness of divine judgment
Term names deep inner perplexity — the state of being thoroughly at a loss before spiritual or extraordinary reality
Term names the inner state of perplexity — being at a loss, uncertain, or disturbed without being driven to despair
Term names the inner state of perplexity and bewilderment — the person at a complete loss before an inexplicable event
Term names reckless instability of character — the inner disposition that acts impulsively without regard for moral boundaries
